tata.FY2023-24: AMBIGUOUS: FY2023 is consolidated while FY2024 is standalone

sections.about: This metric measures construction and demolition waste generated under BRSR Principle 6, Essential Indicator on category-wise waste generation. It is typically linked to project activity, plant upgrades, civil works and demolition rather than routine steelmaking. A lower value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: Tata reported 1,714 MT in FY2025, while JSW, SAIL and Jindal all reported zero. Tata improved from 3,061 MT in FY2024, but remained the only company with reported C&D waste in FY2025. SAIL’s one-year FY2024 spike of 9,063 MT reversed to zero in FY2025, indicating project timing rather than structural operating intensity.
sections.tataPositioning: Tata ranked 4th in FY2025. Because all peers reported zero, a percentage gap is not meaningful; Tata’s absolute gap is 1,714 MT. Tata’s absolute performance improved year-on-year, but rank worsened because peers reported zero.
sections.targets: No C&D-waste-specific target was identified in the retrieved extracts.
sections.comparabilityNotes: This metric is project-cycle sensitive and should not be interpreted as a pure process-efficiency metric. Tata Steel FY2022-23 data is reported on a consolidated basis rather than standalone.

flags.flagNote: Tata FY2023-to-FY2024 trend is ambiguous; zero-peer comparison prevents meaningful percentage gap.
